programmes.show_name: Rhod Gilbert: The Book of John
programmes.show_description: Raw, personal and brutally honest, award-winning comedian Rhod takes us on a no-nonsense journey into finding humour when you've hit rock-bottom. Recorded at the Wales Millennium Centre.
Bill demonstrates the influence of Cockney music on the classical greats and warns of a world ruled by insects, in his debut live stand-up show at the Bloomsbury Theatre, back in 1996.
Diwan: Gordon helps an Indian restaurant in New York that's been going for 35 years. But a decline in trade has led to fusion experiments - with mixed results.
Fiona and John hope that their guests will embrace their green ethos at Knockaloe Beg Farm on the Isle of Man. Rivals Robbie and John are pleasantly surprised by the cleanliness.
Ex-demolition man Phil Routledge hopes to blow his guests away at the ultra-modern The Arches on the Isle of Man. But some of his high-tech gadgets leave them confused.
Can Robbie and John's traditional Blackpool B&B, The Trafford, impress their well-heeled rivals? The hosts try to sweeten the deal with a lesson in how to make Blackpool rock.
Empty-nester Julie Brogan welcomes her rivals to the grand Sheepy Lodge in Warwickshire, where she faces her worst fear: cooking poached eggs for her guests.
It's the final day of the competition, and the rival B&Bers meet to settle some scores and to find out how much they've been paid - and who's won the week.
Some extra brunch as Tim Lovejoy and Simon Rimmer host the food and chat show.
With the chapel still uninhabitable, Keith and Marj work on their first on-site bedroom: a shipping container in the car park!
Inner Tube: After playing mud football, Doug dozes off in front of the TV and has some unusual dreams.
Papa Pill: Doug regrets letting Arthur go to a cocktail lounge.
Package Deal: Thinking he's been offered a job at FedEx, Doug tries to renegotiate his package at work. Meanwhile, Carrie overdoes it with the fake tan.
Frinkcoin: Professor Frink develops a cryptocurrency that makes him rich and gets him new friends. But are they true friends?
Todd, Todd, Why Hast Thou Forsaken Me?: Todd loses his faith in God. Homer and Ned share a near-death experience.
Day 1 begins with the Para Alpine Downhill including Neil Simpson, the first British male to win gold on the snow. And will the GB Wheelchair Curling Mixed Team brush Slovakia aside?
Day 1 continues as Scott Meenagh competes in his third and final Paralympics. Plus, after fighting to be fit, can Menna Fitzpatrick make her mark on the Alpine Skiing Downhill?
...Highlights: F1 is back! As well as each other, the drivers must contend with new regulations. Oh, and there's a new team on the grid. But who has the early-season pace in Melbourne?
Radzi Chinyanganya and Clare Balding present all the action, as Claudia Winkleman roves the NEC seeking out the latest hot dog news. Plus: make some noise for the Flyball quarter-finals.

programmes.show_name: War in Middle East C4 News Special
programmes.show_description: Channel 4's flagship news programme. Including the weather.
Ade Adepitan rounds up the best of Day 1. Did Menna Fitzpatrick or Neil Simpson achieve a 'piste de résistance' performance in the Downhill Alpine Skiing?
Claudia Winkleman joins Clare Balding for a star-studded night including judging of the Utility and Toy Groups. And it's not strictly ballroom, but it is Heelwork to Music. Plus: Scruffts.
In Ho Chi Minh City, the most populous city in Vietnam, Bill is caught between wartime tunnels, endless crazy traffic, and a deep dive into a local musical twist of metal versus folk. (Ep2)
...Harold Fry: (2023) Rachel Joyce's poignant drama starring Jim Broadbent. A retired man walks from Devon to Berwick to save a former colleague from cancer, using nothing but faith.
(2014) World War II action with Brad Pitt. A tank commander leads his crew into the dark, bloody heart of Hitler's last stand. But there's a rookie on board the Fury. With Michael Peña.
Could you survive being handcuffed to a total stranger 24/7? Jonathan Ross challenges 18 brave Brits to stay together, as they compete to win a £100k prize. (Ep1)
Could you stay handcuffed to a stranger 24/7? Self-confessed prude Charlie is hooked to heavily tattooed Rob. Can Christian hairdresser Nina handle Sara's chatty nature? (Ep2)
Kirstie and Phil catch up with cautious first-time buyers Sundeep and Rupreet in Harrow and postcode-picky Hilary and Rob, who set their hearts on Hitchin in Hertfordshire.
The Front: At his high school reunion, Homer wins several awards, including 'Most Weight Gained' and 'Most Improved Odour'. But then the Principal finds out he never graduated.
Brother from the Same Planet: When Homer is late picking up Bart from a school football game, Bart gets a surrogate dad from an agency.
Separation Anxiety: Deacon's upset when Kelly goes on a date.
Departure Time: Doug's sister is jealous when their parents ask Doug to sign a document giving him responsibility to decide when to 'pull the plug' if they ever get very ill.
Hail to the Teeth: Marge's old suitor, Artie Ziff, returns. And he's engaged. Someone tells Lisa she'd be more popular if she smiled more, so she - reluctantly - tries it out.
The Miseducation of Lisa Simpson: Springfield steals the Sea Captain's treasure to build an academy. But Lisa and Bart discover terrifying secret plans for the new school.
Livin' La Pura Vida: Marge's dream multi-family holiday in Costa Rica is ruined when Homer discovers that he gets along with Patty's new girlfriend.
It's Day 2, as GB face Canada in the Wheelchair Mixed Team Curling, and the Men's and Women's Snowboard Cross finals begin.
The excitement continues on Day 2 as the Men's Snowboard Cross heats up. And Nina Sparks hits the fresh Italian snow; can she secure a spot on the podium at her first ever Paralympics?
The grid returns to Albert Park for the opening race of 2026. With new regulations ushering in the next era of F1, who will come out on top down under? Plus: there's a new team.
Claudia Winkleman joins Clare Balding as the excitement builds. The Flyball is wide open with the reigning champions absent. Plus: Intermediate and Large dogs face the Agility course.

programmes.show_name: War in Middle East C4 News Special
programmes.show_description: Channel 4's flagship news programme. Including the weather.
Join Ade Adepitan on Day 2 for all the best action from the Snowboard Cross finals. And which team has the Curling cohesiveness to sweep through the third round?
This is it. Clare Balding is joined by Claudia Winkleman for Best in Show. But first, there's the Gundog Group judging and Hero Dogs. And which breeds look most like our presenting team?

programmes.show_name: New: The Great Pottery Throw Down
programmes.show_description: The Final: The finalists create mini model theatres and tackle a tricky throwing technique. Whose final curtain call will crown them as champion?
This time the Goggleboxers watch The Apprentice, Britain's Got Talent, Handcuffed: Last Pair Standing, Love Is Blind, Celebrity Puzzling, and Alexander Armstrong in India.
